rules.rule: An Introduction should move from necessary context to the specific knowledge gap and end with an explicit aim or review question aligned to the task rubric.
rules.failure_mode: Background summary lacks a clear gap or aim, making the section hard to grade against purpose or rationale criteria.
rules.limitations: Exact order and wording depend on teacher rubric and discipline conventions.

rules.rule: Opening rationale should not imply clinical or mechanistic certainty that the draft's evidence base cannot support.
rules.failure_mode: The Introduction promises novelty, therapy impact, or mechanism beyond the methods/results scope.
rules.limitations: Requires task-specific source evidence to judge biomedical accuracy.

rules.rule: Methods review must check whether a competent reader could reproduce the design, sample selection, materials, software, parameters, databases, versions, thresholds, and statistical analysis.
rules.failure_mode: Methods say what was done at a high level but omit versions, thresholds, accession IDs, or analysis parameters.
rules.limitations: Some dissertations may not require journal-level reproducibility, but omissions should still be flagged as scientific risk.

rules.rule: Select reporting guidelines by study design, not by disease topic; for example STROBE for observational studies, CONSORT for randomized trials, PRISMA for systematic reviews, REMARK for tumor marker prognostic studies.
rules.failure_mode: Reviewer applies PRISMA to a non-systematic narrative background or applies cancer biomarker expectations to all oncology writing.
rules.limitations: Actual applicability must be confirmed during specialization.

rules.rule: Results should present findings, numerical evidence, figure/table references, and statistical uncertainty before broad interpretation.
rules.failure_mode: Results read like Discussion, with mechanism or implication claims not anchored to displayed data.
rules.limitations: Some thesis chapters integrate short interpretation, but evidence must remain distinguishable from inference.

rules.rule: Results review should check whether numerical findings can be extracted and matched to methods, figures, tables, sample sizes, and statistical tests.
rules.failure_mode: Claimed differences lack effect sizes, sample sizes, uncertainty, or test names needed to interpret strength.
rules.limitations: Exact reporting requirements depend on study design and rubric.

rules.rule: Discussion should interpret findings in relation to aims, prior evidence, limitations, and plausible alternative explanations.
rules.failure_mode: Discussion repeats results or overstates translational significance without limitations or alternative explanations.
rules.limitations: Depth and ordering depend on dissertation rubric and available evidence.

rules.rule: The Discussion may synthesize and interpret, but should not introduce major new results or unsupported claims absent from Results and cited evidence.
rules.failure_mode: A major mechanistic or clinical claim appears for the first time in Discussion.
rules.limitations: Literature context is allowed, but new claims must be clearly sourced.

rules.rule: Figure legends should identify what each panel shows, sample/group definitions, key methods or measurements, statistics, abbreviations, and any scale or normalization needed to interpret the figure.
rules.failure_mode: Legend names the panel but omits groups, test, units, or meaning of symbols.
rules.limitations: Journal or dissertation formatting rules may constrain legend length.

rules.rule: Bioinformatics review should check code, data, accession IDs, repository links, software versions, dependency context, parameters, and test data availability.
rules.failure_mode: Pipeline cannot be reproduced because data accessions, software versions, scripts, or parameters are missing.
rules.limitations: Dissertations may have institutional privacy constraints; mark unavailable items and ask for allowed description.

rules.rule: ASCReview must treat teacher-rubric alignment as the first priority only after a real rubric has been supplied; before then, this rule is an input gate and must not create teacher-specific criteria or scores.
rules.failure_mode: Reviewer invents grading criteria or claims rubric alignment before the user supplies a marking scheme.
rules.limitations: Generic build cannot know future criteria; specialization must extract them from a supplied rubric before scoring.

rules.rule: Scientific claims should be calibrated to evidence type, sample size, design limits, statistical support, and whether evidence is direct, indirect, preliminary, or speculative.
rules.failure_mode: Draft turns association into causation, in vitro evidence into clinical promise, or exploratory analysis into confirmation.
rules.limitations: Requires domain-specific evidence review during specialization.

rules.rule: A revision-round issue is resolved only when the revised draft visibly fixes the underlying rubric or scientific problem, not merely when wording changes.
rules.failure_mode: Feedback is marked resolved because a sentence changed, but the missing evidence, logic gap, or rubric criterion remains.
rules.limitations: Requires access to previous feedback and revised text.

rules.rule: When the task involves a defined study type, the Introduction should prepare the reader for that study type by naming the rationale for the design, population/model/data type, and outcome or phenomenon without importing the full reporting checklist into the Introduction.
rules.failure_mode: Introduction gives disease background but does not explain why the chosen design or evidence type is appropriate, leaving Methods to appear arbitrary.
rules.limitations: Only applies when study type is known during specialization; do not infer design from topic alone.

rules.rule: If a project relies on public genomics or cancer-portal data, the rationale should distinguish discovery, exploratory analysis, validation, and clinical inference rather than implying that portal-accessible associations are automatically mechanistic or clinically actionable.
rules.failure_mode: The Introduction treats public-database availability or alteration frequency as sufficient proof of mechanism, prognosis, or therapeutic actionability.
rules.limitations: Optional cancer-genomics seed only; use for non-cancer public datasets by analogy only after specialization.

rules.rule: For in vivo animal studies, Methods review should check study design, sample-size rationale, inclusion/exclusion criteria, randomization, blinding, outcome measures, statistical methods, animal details, and experimental procedures against ARRIVE 2.0 where relevant.
rules.failure_mode: Animal-model Methods report intervention and assay names but omit bias controls, animal characteristics, allocation, exclusions, or outcome definitions.
rules.limitations: Apply only to in vivo animal experiments; not relevant to cell-line-only, computational-only, or purely literature tasks.

rules.rule: For human biospecimen studies, Methods review should check whether relevant preanalytical variables are reported, including biospecimen type, anatomical site, disease/clinical/pathology context, collection, stabilization, preservation, and known missing biospecimen metadata.
rules.failure_mode: Assay or biomarker results are interpreted without enough specimen context to judge sample comparability or preanalytical bias.
rules.limitations: Not every BRISQ item applies to every study; unknown items should be acknowledged rather than treated as automatic fatal flaws.

rules.rule: Methods review should check traceability of key resources such as cell lines, antibodies, model organisms, software, databases, and source data using identifiers, catalog numbers, RRIDs, accessions, versions, or stable URLs where relevant.
rules.failure_mode: The draft names a reagent, cell line, antibody, database, or script without enough identifier detail for another reader to identify the same resource.
rules.limitations: Exact identifier expectations depend on discipline, journal/rubric, and whether the resource is central to the claims.

rules.rule: Data and code availability review should distinguish public repository deposition, controlled-access data, third-party restrictions, privacy/ethical limits, and genuinely unavailable materials, and should require clear conditions of access rather than vague availability claims.
rules.failure_mode: A data availability statement says data are available on request or uses a public dataset without identifiers, access conditions, or restrictions.
rules.limitations: Institutional privacy and licensing constraints can be legitimate; classify the issue as traceability/access risk rather than misconduct.

rules.rule: Results should make denominators, analyzed sample counts, exclusions, missing data, and group definitions visible when these affect interpretation of an experiment, cohort, animal study, or computational analysis.
rules.failure_mode: Figures or result paragraphs report effects without making clear how many samples, animals, patients, observations, or features were included and excluded.
rules.limitations: Exact denominator format depends on study design and task conventions.

rules.rule: Result claims that depend on figures, plots, or large datasets should be traceable to underlying data values, accession IDs, supplementary tables, repositories, or code/data availability statements when those materials are central to verification.
rules.failure_mode: A graph supports the main conclusion but the plotted values, source table, dataset accession, or generation script cannot be found.
rules.limitations: Some dissertation drafts may not yet have final repositories; flag as missing information or pre-submission risk.

rules.rule: For animal or preclinical model studies, Discussion should state model-specific limits, bias risks, imprecision, and whether findings are exploratory or hypothesis-testing before making translational implications.
rules.failure_mode: The Discussion turns an animal, cell, or exploratory result into a broad clinical implication without model limitations or bias caveats.
rules.limitations: Apply only when preclinical models are part of the task; human clinical evidence needs different guidelines.

rules.rule: For diagnostic, prognostic, or AI/ML prediction models, Discussion should distinguish development, internal validation, external validation, calibration/discrimination performance, clinical utility, and generalizability limits.
rules.failure_mode: A model's apparent performance is discussed as clinically useful without validation context, calibration, external applicability, or deployment limits.
rules.limitations: Use only when prediction-model work is in scope; not every machine-learning analysis is a clinical prediction model.

rules.rule: Figure and legend review should check that central plotted data or resource-dependent panels are linked to source data, resource identifiers, dataset accessions, or code provenance when needed to verify the claim.
rules.failure_mode: A plot, image, or multi-panel figure cannot be traced to the underlying data, resource, or analysis script.
rules.limitations: Not every illustrative schematic needs source-data provenance; apply to evidence-bearing panels.

rules.rule: Figures using statistical comparisons should define group sizes, error bars or intervals, tests, correction approach where relevant, and symbols/thresholds in the legend or associated Methods.
rules.failure_mode: A figure uses asterisks, brackets, bars, or shaded intervals without explaining comparison groups, uncertainty, or statistical meaning.
rules.limitations: Legend length constraints may move details to Methods, but the reader still needs a clear decoding path.

rules.rule: RNA-seq differential-expression review should check count source, sample/replicate structure, design formula or comparison model, normalization, dispersion/fold-change handling, filtering thresholds, multiple-testing correction, and software/package version.
rules.failure_mode: Differential-expression claims are reported without enough design, normalization, threshold, or software detail to judge reproducibility or statistical validity.
rules.limitations: DESeq2 is a source pattern, not a mandated tool; analogous reporting applies to edgeR, limma-voom, and other appropriate methods.

rules.rule: Public cancer-genomics portal analyses should report portal/database, study ID or cohort, data release or access date, molecular profile selected, gene/set query, filters, clinical endpoint definitions, and whether the analysis is exploratory or validated.
rules.failure_mode: A draft cites a portal-derived survival or alteration association without specifying cohort, query settings, endpoint, filters, or validation status.
rules.limitations: Applies to public portal analyses generally; platform-specific fields must be specialized to the portal used.

rules.rule: When no teacher rubric is present, official guidelines and journal standards may identify scientific or reporting risks, but they must not be converted into score penalties or teacher-specific criteria.
rules.failure_mode: ASCReview invents a grading scheme by treating PRISMA, ARRIVE, journal author instructions, or reporting standards as if they were the user's marking scheme.
rules.limitations: Future teacher rubrics may explicitly incorporate guidelines; specialization must detect that case.

rules.rule: A revision round should check whether new text, figures, datasets, analyses, or claims introduced during revision created new provenance, availability, or traceability gaps.
rules.failure_mode: A revised section fixes an old wording problem but adds a new dataset, figure, analysis, or claim without source, method, or availability information.
rules.limitations: Requires comparison against previous draft and knowledge of what changed.

rules.rule: Translational biomedical claims should identify whether support comes from in vitro data, animal models, biospecimen/observational association, computational public-data analysis, prediction modeling, or clinical intervention evidence, and should not leap across evidence levels without caveats.
rules.failure_mode: A draft treats preclinical, biospecimen, public-data, or prediction-model findings as immediate clinical actionability.
rules.limitations: Requires task-specific domain evidence and cannot alone determine biomedical truth.

rules.rule: For routinely collected health data, registry, or database analyses, Methods review should check data source, linkage logic, code/algorithm definitions, inclusion/exclusion logic, cleaning steps, access conditions, and whether the data were collected for the research question or repurposed.
rules.failure_mode: A registry or electronic-record analysis reports associations without source-system provenance, linkage/phenotype algorithm detail, or missingness/cleaning transparency.
rules.limitations: RECORD is a STROBE extension for routinely collected health data; do not apply it to all observational work.

rules.rule: For qPCR or RT-qPCR experiments, Methods review should check sample handling, nucleic-acid quantity/quality, primer/probe identity, assay conditions, normalization/reference strategy, and enough reaction detail to judge technical validity.
rules.failure_mode: A draft reports qPCR validation or expression results without primer/probe information, RNA quality, normalization rationale, or assay-condition details.
rules.limitations: MIQE source is metadata-only in this corpus until reuse is verified; use as guideline routing support, not stored excerpt content.

rules.rule: When sex, gender, or sex-derived biological material is relevant to study design or interpretation, Results review should check whether groups, exclusions, disaggregation, and negative as well as positive sex/gender analyses are reported or justified.
rules.failure_mode: A human, animal, cell, or tissue study draws general conclusions while hiding sex/gender composition or failing to explain why sex/gender analysis was not relevant.
rules.limitations: Apply only when sex/gender relevance can be established from task context; absence is not automatically fatal in irrelevant or underpowered settings.

rules.rule: Results based on public genomics repositories should identify repository, accession/study ID, data release or access date, sample/case counts, query filters, endpoint definitions, and whether the plotted or tested values are raw, normalized, harmonized, or portal-derived.
rules.failure_mode: A result cites GEO, GDC, GENIE, TCGA, or cBioPortal without enough query/provenance detail to reproduce the cohort, profile, or plotted values.
rules.limitations: Portal-specific required fields vary; specialization should adapt the checklist to the actual repository.

rules.rule: Discussion of real-world, registry, or public clinico-genomic data should distinguish hypothesis generation from validation, name selection/missingness/linkage biases, and avoid implying treatment efficacy or clinical actionability without appropriate design evidence.
rules.failure_mode: A public registry association is discussed as though it proves mechanism, treatment response, or clinical utility without design and bias caveats.
rules.limitations: Cancer registry sources can support translational reasoning but cannot replace trial, functional, or external-validation evidence.

rules.rule: Genomics and bioinformatics figures should make QC, normalization, sample grouping, scale, transformation, thresholds, and data provenance decodable from the legend, panel labels, Methods, or supplement.
rules.failure_mode: A heatmap, volcano plot, pathway plot, survival curve, or genome-browser panel lacks enough legend/method detail to interpret scale, filtering, cohort, or QC status.
rules.limitations: SEQC and ENCODE support assay/QC logic but not universal thresholds across all genomics modalities.

rules.rule: RNA-seq methods review should check library/sample design, read QC, alignment or pseudoalignment strategy, quantification unit, annotation version, normalization, filtering, batch/covariate handling, multiple-testing correction, and visualization/diagnostic checks.
rules.failure_mode: A draft reports differential expression or transcriptomic enrichment without describing QC, annotation, normalization, covariates, or diagnostic evidence.
rules.limitations: This is a reporting checklist, not a prescribed pipeline; appropriate methods differ by design, platform, and question.

rules.rule: Model-based omics analyses should state the design matrix or model structure, contrasts/comparisons, covariates or blocking factors, batch handling, and how the tested hypothesis maps to the biological question.
rules.failure_mode: A gene-list or pathway result is presented without explaining which comparison was modeled, which covariates were adjusted, or how contrasts map to the claim.
rules.limitations: Applies to model-based omics analyses; not every descriptive analysis has a design matrix.

rules.rule: Data, code, and materials availability gaps should be framed as reproducibility, verification, or policy-compliance risks unless there is direct evidence of false reporting or misconduct.
rules.failure_mode: A review escalates missing repository links or unclear access conditions into accusations of misconduct instead of a precise reproducibility limitation.
rules.limitations: If a draft explicitly makes a false availability statement, the issue can be escalated as a factual inconsistency; evidence is required.

rules.rule: When a revision changes datasets, repository accessions, preprocessing, model design, contrasts, thresholds, or figure-generation code, the reviewer should check whether downstream results, legends, methods, and claims were updated consistently.
rules.failure_mode: A revision updates the analysis pipeline or public-data query but leaves old figure legends, sample counts, thresholds, or claims in place.
rules.limitations: Requires previous and current drafts plus enough change history to identify analysis drift.

rules.rule: Methods review should route diagnostic accuracy work to diagnostic reporting checks and intervention or comparator descriptions to intervention-description checks, but only after the study design confirms those scopes.
rules.failure_mode: A diagnostic test or intervention study lacks reference-standard/index-test detail, comparator/intervention detail, fidelity, or replication information, or the reviewer applies STARD/TIDieR to a non-diagnostic/non-intervention project.
rules.limitations: STARD and TIDieR are design-specific; they must not create generic dissertation scoring criteria.

rules.rule: Single-cell RNA-seq review should check sample and library provenance, cell/barcode filtering, QC covariates, doublet or ambient RNA handling where relevant, normalization, batch/integration strategy, clustering or cell annotation evidence, and marker or differential-expression testing.
rules.failure_mode: A single-cell result reports clusters, markers, trajectories, or cell-type claims without QC/filtering, annotation evidence, or analysis-object provenance.
rules.limitations: This is not a mandate to use Scanpy, Seurat, or any one workflow; acceptable details depend on platform and question.

rules.rule: Spatial transcriptomics review should check tissue/image context, spot or cell resolution, spatial coordinates, segmentation or deconvolution method, normalization/QC, spatial statistics or domain-detection method, integration with single-cell references where used, and limitations of resolution or model assumptions.
rules.failure_mode: A spatial transcriptomics claim describes cell neighborhoods, spatial domains, or cell-cell interaction without image/coordinate, resolution, segmentation/deconvolution, or model-provenance detail.
rules.limitations: Spatial-specific checks do not apply to ordinary bulk RNA-seq or non-spatial single-cell analyses.

rules.rule: Proteomics and proteogenomics review should check repository or study accession, sample metadata, acquisition/processing workflow, instrument/search parameters where relevant, raw and processed file availability, protein/peptide inference level, and controlled-access or license constraints.
rules.failure_mode: A proteomics or proteogenomics result cites PRIDE, ProteomeXchange, CPTAC, or PDC but lacks accession, sample metadata, processing provenance, or raw/processed data traceability.
rules.limitations: Repository deposition alone does not prove analytic reproducibility; method details and metadata remain necessary.

rules.rule: Pathway or gene-set enrichment review should check gene universe/background, ranking or threshold strategy, database/resource and version, multiple-testing correction, redundancy handling, directionality, and whether interpretation is exploratory, supportive, or mechanistically validated.
rules.failure_mode: A draft reports enriched pathways as mechanism without background set, database version, correction, directionality, or independent validation.
rules.limitations: Pathway knowledge bases are incomplete and biased; enrichment helps interpretation but does not establish mechanism alone.

rules.rule: Results that rely on embeddings, clusters, spatial domains, heatmaps, pathway plots, or proteogenomic summaries should make the plotted object, sample/cell/feature counts, transformations, grouping, and key analysis choices traceable to Methods or source data.
rules.failure_mode: A UMAP, spatial map, heatmap, pathway dot plot, or proteomic summary is treated as self-explanatory despite missing object provenance, feature/sample counts, or transformation details.
rules.limitations: Some details can reside in Methods, supplement, or source-data tables; the Results text still needs a clear trace path.

rules.rule: Single-cell, spatial, pathway, and proteomics figures should decode embedding axes, clustering or annotation labels, spatial/image registration, color scales, thresholds, feature sets, sample/cell counts, and data/object provenance.
rules.failure_mode: A visually persuasive embedding, spatial plot, pathway plot, or proteomics figure lacks enough legend detail to decode what was plotted and how it was generated.
rules.limitations: Legends may cross-reference Methods or supplements; the decoding path must still be explicit.

rules.rule: Pan-cancer, cross-tumor, or public consortium analyses should discuss tumor-type heterogeneity, cohort and platform differences, open versus controlled data tiers, driver-discovery incompleteness, and validation needs before generalizing mechanisms or clinical implications.
rules.failure_mode: A draft generalizes a pan-cancer association or pathway signal across cancers without addressing tumor lineage, platform, cohort, data-access, or validation constraints.
rules.limitations: Pan-cancer sources are generic cancer-genomics examples and must not hardcode any user dissertation topic.

rules.rule: Pathway enrichment, gene-set association, clustering, spatial neighborhood, or proteogenomic correlation should not be worded as demonstrated mechanism unless supported by direct functional, perturbational, causal, or independently validated evidence.
rules.failure_mode: A draft treats an enriched pathway, cluster marker, spatial co-localization, or proteogenomic association as causal mechanism or therapeutic actionability.
rules.limitations: Associational omics patterns can support hypotheses; they do not by themselves adjudicate biological truth.

rules.rule: Before a dissertation, thesis, article, proposal, or revision review begins, ASCReview must record the status of draft, marking scheme, figures/assets, source constraints, and assessment context, and must disable scoring, figure review, factual review, or final export when the relevant input is missing.
rules.failure_mode: A review starts scoring or making figure/domain findings without the materials needed to justify that review mode.
rules.limitations: A user may explicitly permit non-scored formative review, but the report must label the permission and remaining gaps.

rules.rule: Single-cell multimodal review should route checks by modality, including RNA, chromatin accessibility, surface-protein tags, immune receptor repertoire, and spatial context, and should identify whether modalities are paired from the same cells or integrated across separate datasets.
rules.failure_mode: A multimodal single-cell result reports integrated clusters or joint biology without explaining which modalities were measured, paired, normalized, aligned, or independently benchmarked.
rules.limitations: This rule is a routing check; it must not require one integration algorithm or imply that all single-cell studies are multimodal.

rules.rule: Adaptive immune receptor repertoire review should check sample and subject metadata, receptor chain and locus, library and primer strategy, sequence annotation fields, germline reference/database context, repertoire-level summaries, repository or AIRR-compliant representation, and analysis tool provenance.
rules.failure_mode: An AIRR-seq result reports clonotypes, diversity, convergence, or disease signatures without enough sample, annotation, germline-reference, or tool provenance to assess interpretation.
rules.limitations: AIRR checks apply only to adaptive immune receptor repertoire data, not to ordinary immune-cell marker expression or all immunology studies.

rules.rule: Long-read sequencing review should check platform and chemistry, basecalling version, read length and quality distribution, yield/coverage, error-correction or polishing strategy, alignment or assembly method, variant/isoform/base-modification caller, benchmark or validation evidence, and limitations caused by error profile or throughput.
rules.failure_mode: A long-read result reports structural variants, assemblies, isoforms, methylation, or haplotypes without platform-specific QC, basecalling, error-correction, coverage, or validation context.
rules.limitations: Long reads are not automatically preferable for every genomics question; platform and analysis choices must match the claim.

rules.rule: Mass-spectrometry proteomics review should identify acquisition mode and quantification strategy, distinguishing DDA, DIA/SWATH, targeted methods, labeling, fractionation, spectral-library or library-free analysis, false-discovery control, missingness handling, and peptide/protein inference level.
rules.failure_mode: A proteomics result reports differential abundance or pathway interpretation without acquisition mode, search/scoring workflow, quantification strategy, FDR, missingness, or inference-level detail.
rules.limitations: DIA/SWATH guidance must not be imposed on DDA, targeted, TMT, or other proteomics designs; route by acquisition mode.

rules.rule: Phosphoproteomics review should check enrichment and sample-preparation strategy, acquisition and search workflow, phosphorylation-site localization confidence, normalization to protein abundance where relevant, kinase or pathway inference method, multiplicity correction, and whether site-level signals are experimentally validated.
rules.failure_mode: A draft interprets phosphorylation abundance, kinase enrichment, or signaling pathway activity without site localization, protein-level normalization, inference method, or validation caveats.
rules.limitations: Phosphoproteomic association can suggest signaling hypotheses but does not alone prove causal pathway activation.

rules.rule: Highly multiplexed tissue imaging figures should make tissue/sample context, markers/channels, acquisition platform, image processing, segmentation or cell-calling, spatial coordinate/region definitions, color mapping, scale, and source-data provenance traceable through legends, Methods, supplement, or repository metadata.
rules.failure_mode: A multiplexed tissue image panel shows neighborhoods, marker states, or spatial patterns without channel, segmentation, scale, region, or image-derived-data provenance.
rules.limitations: MITI-style checks apply to tissue imaging and related imaging-based spatial assays, not to every ordinary microscopy figure.

rules.rule: Revision-round comparison requires at least a revised draft plus prior feedback or previous review record, and should also record whether the previous draft, prior score basis, response notes, figures/assets, and source constraints are available before classifying resolution or score movement.
rules.failure_mode: A revision review declares issues resolved, unresolved, or score-improving without access to the prior feedback or revised material needed for comparison.
rules.limitations: A high-level revision plan can still be prepared without full materials, but resolution classification and score movement must be blocked or provisional.

rules.rule: Figure review must record whether figure assets, legends/captions, source data, and the citing Results text are available; visual-readability, panel-level, or source-data findings must be blocked or marked provisional when the relevant material is missing.
rules.failure_mode: A review criticizes visual design, panel readability, statistics, or figure-text alignment based only on figure mentions or absent assets.
rules.limitations: Text-only figure-reference checks may proceed if the user explicitly asks for them, but visual and panel-level findings require actual assets or sufficiently detailed descriptions.
